merged_events.event_description: First U.S. Consul to Greece (Gregory A. Perdicaris) took up his post at Athens, establishing consular relations in the capital.

evidence.quote: On June 27, 1837, the United States appointed Gregory A. Perdicaris to be its first Consul to Greece. He took up his post at Athens on January 7, 1838.

merged_events.event_description: Charles K. Tuckerman, the first U.S. Minister Resident in Athens, presented his credentials, establishing the American Legation in Greece.

evidence.quote: On March 11, 1868, the United States appointed Charles K. Tuckerman as its first Minister Resident in Athens. He presented his credentials on June 16, 1868.

merged_events.event_description: The United States closed its Legation in Athens due to the German occupation of Greece.

evidence.quote: The United State closed its Legation in Athens on July 14, 1941, due to the German occupation of Greece.

merged_events.event_description: Anthony J. Drexel Biddle, Jr. was appointed Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Greece, serving near the Government of Greece in exile in England, re-establishing a legation-level presence.

evidence.quote: Anthony J. Drexel Biddle, Jr., who was appointed Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Greece on November 13, 1941, served near the Government of Greece established in England.

merged_events.event_description: The American Legation was elevated to Embassy status when Anthony J. Drexel Biddle, Jr. was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to the Government of Greece in exile.

evidence.quote: The United States promoted Anthony J. Drexel Biddle, Jr., to be the new Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to the Government of Greece on September 29, 1942.

merged_events.event_description: Normal diplomatic relations with Greece were interrupted, ending the U.S. Legation's active operations.

evidence.quote: Edward Capps, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1920-06-18. Credentials presented: 1920-10-11. Normal relations interrupted on 1920-12-06.

merged_events.event_description: Jefferson Caffery, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, presented credentials, resuming U.S. diplomatic representation in Greece at the legation level.

evidence.quote: Jefferson Caffery, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1922-02. Left post: 1923-05.

merged_events.event_description: Anthony J. Drexel Biddle Jr. presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary near the Government of Greece in exile in England, re-establishing the U.S. Legation.

evidence.quote: Anthony Joseph Drexel Jr Biddle, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1941-11-13. Credentials presented: 1941-11-28. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1942-10-30. Note: Served near the Government of Greece established in England.

merged_events.event_description: Biddle was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presented credentials, elevating the U.S. mission to Greece from a Legation to an Embassy.

evidence.quote: Anthony Joseph Drexel Jr Biddle, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1942-09-29. Credentials presented: 1942-10-30. Government of Greece transferred to Egypt on 1943-03-16.

matched.notes: Exact match on date (1838-01-07) and status (Consulate). RDCR confirms Perdicaris took up his post at Athens on this date.
Exact match on date (1868-06-16) and status (Legation). RDCR confirms Tuckerman presented credentials on this date.

CSV date 1942-10-30 matches POCOM Event 8 (credential presentation date). RDCR Event 4 gives 1942-09-29 but this is a promotion/appointment date, not an institutional date. Per source priority rules, POCOM wins when RDCR only provides a personnel date. CSV correctly follows POCOM here.

missing_from_csv.event_description: The United States closed its Legation in Athens due to the German occupation of Greece.
missing_from_csv.notes: High-confidence RDCR event. This closure is a significant status change (Legation → None) that should precede the re-establishment of the Legation near the government in exile. Strong candidate for addition.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Anthony J. Drexel Biddle, Jr. was appointed Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Greece, serving near the Government of Greece in exile in England, re-establishing legation-level relations.
missing_from_csv.notes: RDCR gives appointment date 1941-11-13; POCOM gives credential presentation date 1941-11-28. RDCR's date is an appointment/personnel date, not an explicit institutional date (no statement like 'the Legation was re-established on...'). POCOM's credential presentation date (1941-11-28) may be more appropriate for the institutional re-establishment. Either way, this event is absent from the CSV and represents the resumption of Legation-level relations after the 1941-07-14 closure.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Normal diplomatic relations with Greece were interrupted, ending the U.S. Legation's active operations.
missing_from_csv.notes: POCOM-only event (high confidence). Edward Capps' record notes 'Normal relations interrupted on 1920-12-06.' This likely relates to the political crisis in Greece following the return of King Constantine. Candidate for addition, though only supported by POCOM.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Jefferson Caffery, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, presented credentials, resuming U.S. diplomatic representation in Greece at the legation level.
missing_from_csv.notes: POCOM-only event (medium confidence). Date is month-level precision only (1922-02). Represents resumption of Legation after the 1920-12-06 interruption. Candidate for addition, though only supported by POCOM.
